name: Nansen
theirModel: API key or subscription for smart-money and wallet analytics.
syraAngle: Syra exposes Nansen-style smart-money netflow as a pay-per-call x402 route and MCP tool — one USDC wallet, no separate Nansen key for agent loops that also need news, DEX, and oracle data.
whenTheyWin: You only need Nansen continuously at enterprise volume with a negotiated contract.
whenSyraWins: Your agent needs many intel sources mid-task without vendor onboarding, and you want HTTP 402 auto-pay.
ctaNote: Try syra_spend_nansen_smart_money_netflow via MCP or the marketplace.

name: CoinGecko API
theirModel: Free tier + paid API keys for market, trending, and price data.
syraAngle: Syra wraps CoinGecko scout and related market intel behind x402 so agents pay per successful call instead of managing another API key and billing account.
whenTheyWin: You only call CoinGecko and already have a key with spare quota.
whenSyraWins: Your agent mixes CoinGecko with on-chain, news, and risk tools under one payer.
ctaNote: Try syra_spend_coingecko_scout via MCP or the marketplace.

name: DefiLlama
theirModel: Mostly free public API for protocol and chain TVL.
syraAngle: Syra packages DefiLlama TVL as an agent-callable x402/MCP tool next to DEX pairs, sentiment, and smart money — useful when TVL is one step in a multi-tool agent plan.
whenTheyWin: You only need raw TVL dumps in a script with no payment rails.
whenSyraWins: An autonomous agent should fetch TVL then act (scout, swap quote, risk) without human key pasting.
ctaNote: Try syra_spend_defillama_tvl via MCP or the marketplace.

name: DexScreener
theirModel: Public pair search and charts; rate limits and key policies vary by use.
syraAngle: Syra offers DexScreener pairs as a paid x402 route with MCP packaging so trading agents can discover liquidity alongside rug checks and oracles on one wallet.
whenTheyWin: Human browsers and dashboards hitting DexScreener directly.
whenSyraWins: Agents that need pairs + risk + price in one paid session.
ctaNote: Try syra_spend_dexscreener_pairs via MCP or the marketplace.

name: Messari
theirModel: Research subscriptions and API access for fundamentals and news-grade intel.
syraAngle: Syra focuses on agent-native pay-per-call crypto intel (news, sentiment, signals, on-chain scouts) with USDC settlement — not a research seat license.
whenTheyWin: Human analysts who need deep research reports and a Messari workflow.
whenSyraWins: Agents that must pay for discrete intel calls and continue the task automatically.
ctaNote: Browse Spend tools on the Syra marketplace.

name: Birdeye
theirModel: API keys for Solana token prices, markets, and wallets.
syraAngle: Syra aggregates partner and first-party Spend routes under x402 so Solana agents avoid N vendor keys; partner passthrough typically prices at ~1.2× upstream.
whenTheyWin: High-volume single-vendor Birdeye usage with an existing key.
whenSyraWins: Multi-tool agents that need Birdeye-class data plus news, risk, and MCP install in one loop.
ctaNote: Explore Solana intel routes on syraa.fun/marketplace.

name: Build-your-own x402 payer
theirModel: Wire each upstream vendor yourself, implement HTTP 402 payment, MCP schemas, and catalog maintenance.
syraAngle: Syra ships the gateway, OpenAPI/x402 catalog, MCP server, and typed SDK so agent builders pay per call instead of rebuilding settlement and tool packaging.
whenTheyWin: You only need one internal route and already own the settlement stack.
whenSyraWins: You want many crypto intel tools with one USDC payer and minutes-to-first-call DX.
ctaNote: Install @syra-ai/mcp-server or @syra-ai/sdk and make a first paid call.

name: Per-vendor API keys
primaryKeyword: pay per call API vs API keys
theirModel: Sign up, get keys, manage quotas and invoices per vendor (Nansen, CoinGecko, news, etc.).
syraAngle: Machine money for agents: settle USDC on HTTP 402, discover routes via OpenAPI and MCP, no key sprawl for the agent runtime.
whenTheyWin: Stable single-vendor production services with procurement already done.
whenSyraWins: Autonomous agents and hackathon builds that cannot pause for vendor dashboards.
ctaNote: Read /pricing and install MCP for a wallet-native path.
